skill.name: formik-patterns
skill.description: Formik form handling patterns for React and React Native forms, including Yup validation, field helpers, submissions, and multi-step flows.
skill.summary: Build Formik forms with validation, submission, edit-state, and multi-step patterns.
skill.author: ChrisWiles

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found these are false positives from Markdown examples and ordinary form text. No executable scripts, network exfiltration, credential access, prompt injection, or malicious intent were found in SKILL.md. The skill is documentation-only and safe to publish with low residual risk.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misclassified as External Commands
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks as Ruby or shell execution, but these locations are fenced TypeScript and TSX documentation examples. No executable hook, shell command, or script file is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown code fence delimiters around form examples. There is no Ruby, shell syntax, executable file, or command invocation in the skill.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Pattern Is a Text Match
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines contain skill description and form field text, not cryptographic algorithms. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, crypto libraries, or hashing operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirms these lines are descriptions or Formik initial values. The file contains no cryptographic API usage or security-sensitive hashing logic.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Pattern Is Form Documentation
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines are validation, UI state, and form submission examples. No evidence found of host enumeration, process inspection, environment discovery, or filesystem reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is React form validation and button state handling. None of the cited lines request system data or perform reconnaissance actions.

content.value_statement: Formik forms often need consistent validation, error handling, and submission state. This skill provides reusable patterns for React and React Native forms using Formik and Yup.
actual_capabilities: Shows a basic Formik setup with Yup validation and controlled inputs.
Provides common Yup schemas for email, password, phone, URLs, numbers, and arrays.
Demonstrates helper patterns for text inputs, selects, and picker fields.
Documents GraphQL mutation submission with success, error, and loading states.
Covers edit forms with initial values, dirty-state checks, and reinitialization.
Includes multi-step form structure and common Formik anti-patterns.
limitations: It is guidance documentation, not a runnable package or code generator.
It assumes Formik, Yup, React, and compatible UI components are already available.
It does not include automated tests or project-specific schema generation.
Examples may need adaptation for each app's navigation, toast, and GraphQL setup.

use_cases.title: Create a validated login form
use_cases.description: Use the basic setup and Yup examples to build email and password forms with touched-state errors.

use_cases.title: Standardize mutation-backed forms
use_cases.description: Apply submission, loading, and error handling patterns for GraphQL create and update workflows.

use_cases.title: Review form implementation quality
use_cases.description: Compare existing forms against the documented anti-patterns for validation display and submit behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Build a simple form
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the formik-patterns skill to create a Formik form for email and password fields with Yup validation and touched-field errors.

prompt_templates.title: Add schema validation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the formik-patterns skill to design a Yup validation schema for this form: [describe fields, required fields, and optional fields].

prompt_templates.title: Improve submit handling
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the formik-patterns skill to refactor this form submission flow so it handles loading, errors, success feedback, and disabled submit state.

prompt_templates.title: Design a multi-step form
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the formik-patterns skill to plan a multi-step Formik flow with separate validation per step and safe final submission.

output_examples.input: Create a registration form with email, password, confirm password, and phone validation.
output: A Formik structure with initial values for all four fields.
A Yup schema covering email format, password complexity, password matching, and phone format.
Field wiring that shows errors only after each field is touched.
A submit button disabled when the form is invalid or submitting.

output_examples.input: Review my edit form that submits a GraphQL update mutation.
output: Recommendations to use initial values from the item record.
Guidance to enable reinitialization when the item prop changes.
A dirty-state check so unchanged forms cannot submit.
Submission feedback for success, failure, and loading states.

best_practices: Show validation errors only after fields are touched or after submission.
Disable submit actions while the form is invalid, unchanged, or submitting.
Wrap async submissions with error handling and always clear submitting state.
anti_patterns: Do not show raw validation errors before the user interacts with a field.
Do not leave submit buttons enabled during invalid or in-flight submissions.
Do not call mutations without visible success and failure handling.

faq.question: Does this skill create runnable code?
faq.answer: It provides implementation patterns and examples. You still need to adapt them to your project.

faq.question: Can it help with React Native forms?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples use input patterns that fit React Native style components.

faq.question: Does it require Yup?
faq.answer: The documented validation patterns use Yup. Other schema libraries would need manual translation.

faq.question: Can it handle GraphQL form submissions?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes create and update mutation patterns with loading and error states.

faq.question: Does it cover multi-step forms?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows a step state pattern with step-specific validation and final submission.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to use?
faq.answer: Yes. Review found only documentation examples and no executable scripts or malicious behavior.
